summary: On Android, SKFontManager.GetFontFamilies() returns font alias names (e.g., 'georgia') that resolve to canonical families (e.g., 'serif') when passed to SKTypeface.FromFamilyName(), because Android's fonts.xml maps most traditional font names as aliases to a small set of actual system fonts.

bugSignals.errorMessage: SKTypeface.FromFamilyName returns typeface with FamilyName 'serif' instead of requested 'georgia'

stepsToReproduce: Call SKFontManager.Default.GetFontFamilies() on Android — returns 'georgia' among others
Call SKTypeface.FromFamilyName('georgia', SKFontStyle) with a name from step 1
Check returned SKTypeface.FamilyName — it returns 'serif' instead of 'georgia'
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: Android 13, .NET 9.0, MAUI 9.0.50, SkiaSharp 3.116.1, Android Emulator (Pixel 5), Galaxy Tab S9+

repoLinks.description: Minimal MAUI repro project from reporter

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: Reporter confirmed the same behavior on 3.119.0 and 2.88.9. The underlying Skia Android font manager code has not changed — aliases are parsed from Android's system fonts.xml.

regression.reason: Reporter initially claimed 2.88.9 as last known good version but later confirmed the same behavior occurs on 2.88.9. This is long-standing Skia behavior on Android, not a SkiaSharp regression.

analysis.summary: This is expected behavior from Skia's Android font manager. Android's fonts.xml defines aliases (e.g., 'georgia' → 'serif') for traditional font names. Skia enumerates these aliases in GetFontFamilies() but the resolved typeface reports its canonical family name from the font file. The disconnect is in Skia's Android port, not SkiaSharp's C# wrapper.
analysis.rationale: Classified as type/bug because the API behavior is confusing — GetFontFamilies() returns names that don't round-trip through FromFamilyName().FamilyName. However, this is upstream Skia behavior on Android and is by-design: Android doesn't ship Georgia, Times New Roman, etc. as real fonts — they're aliases to serif/sans-serif/monospace. Reporter confirmed same behavior on 2.88.9, ruling out regression. The suggest action is close-as-by-design with an explanatory comment and workaround.

keySignals.text: 'georgia' is returned from GetFontFamilies yet both calls to SKTypeface.FromFamilyName return an SKTypeface with FamilyName = 'serif'
keySignals.interpretation: Android alias resolution: 'georgia' maps to 'serif' in fonts.xml, and the typeface reports the canonical name.

keySignals.text: I don't see this issue on Windows and have not tested yet tested on IOS or MacCatalyst
keySignals.interpretation: Windows has real Georgia font installed; Android uses aliases. Platform difference confirms this is Android font system behavior.

keySignals.text: I'm seeing the same behavior with SkiaSharp v3.119.0 and v2.88.9 as well.
keySignals.interpretation: Not a regression — this is long-standing Skia Android behavior across all SkiaSharp versions.

keySignals.text: The only fonts that return the expected FontFamily are the various sans serif/serif fonts. All the others either return sans-serif, sans, or monospace.
keySignals.interpretation: Exactly matches Android's alias structure: only canonical families (serif, sans-serif, monospace) exist as real fonts.

codeInvestigation.finding: onCountFamilies() and onGetFamilyName() enumerate ALL entries in fNameToFamilyMap including aliases, so GetFontFamilies() returns alias names like 'georgia'
Android fonts.xml <alias> elements without weight are added to the existing family's fNames array — e.g., 'georgia' is added to the serif family's names list
addFamily() registers ALL names in FontFamily.fNames to fNameToFamilyMap pointing to the same SkFontStyleSet, so 'georgia' and 'serif' share the same style set
onMatchFamily() uses exact case-insensitive matching against fNameToFamilyMap — 'georgia' is found and returns the serif style set, so the match succeeds but the typeface reports 'serif'
FromFamilyName calls sk_typeface_create_from_name which delegates to SkTypeface::MakeFromName — no SkiaSharp-layer transformation of the result
sk_typeface_create_from_name is a thin wrapper around SkTypeface::MakeFromName — SkiaSharp passes through Skia's result unchanged

workarounds: Bundle the desired font file (e.g., Georgia.ttf) with your app and use SKTypeface.FromFile() or SKTypeface.FromData() to load it directly
Build a mapping table: for each name from GetFontFamilies(), call MatchFamily(name).FamilyName to discover canonical names and filter out aliases
nextQuestions: Should SkiaSharp document the Android alias behavior in the API docs for SKFontManager.GetFontFamilies()?
Could SkiaSharp provide a helper to deduplicate alias names from GetFontFamilies() on Android?

resolution.hypothesis: Android's fonts.xml defines aliases for traditional font names (georgia → serif, arial → sans-serif, etc.). Skia's Android font manager enumerates aliases in GetFontFamilies() but typefaces report their canonical family name. This is upstream Skia behavior, not a SkiaSharp bug.

proposals.title: Bundle fonts with the app
proposals.description: Instead of relying on system font name resolution, bundle the desired .ttf/.otf font files with the MAUI app and load them directly using SKTypeface.FromFile() or SKTypeface.FromData(). This guarantees the exact font is used regardless of platform.

proposals.title: Filter aliases from GetFontFamilies
proposals.description: Build a deduplicated font family list by resolving each name from GetFontFamilies() through MatchFamily() and comparing the returned typeface's FamilyName to the requested name. Only keep entries where they match (canonical names).

resolution.recommendedReason: Bundling fonts is the standard cross-platform approach and guarantees the exact typeface. Relying on system font name resolution is inherently platform-dependent.

actionability.reason: This is upstream Skia behavior reflecting Android's font aliasing system. GetFontFamilies() enumerates aliases by design. The typeface correctly reports its canonical name. Reporter confirmed same behavior on 2.88.9 — not a regression. Workaround exists (bundle fonts).

actions.description: Explain Android font aliasing behavior and provide workarounds
Close as by-design — upstream Skia/Android font aliasing behavior
Cross-reference with font name resolution issue on WASM
